zh.Tittleshall, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可迪特萊沙拉爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Wayte於1488年出生在英國諾福爾可迪特萊沙拉爾時，她的父親William Wayte34歲，母親Margaret29歲。她於從1540年1月11日到1541年1月10日與?在英國諾福爾可結婚。她去世於1566年7月11日在英國諾福爾可享年78歲。

zh-hans.Tittleshall, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可迪特萊沙拉爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Wayte于1488年出生在英国諾福爾可迪特萊沙拉爾时，她的父亲William Wayte34岁，母亲Margaret29岁。她于从1540年1月11日到1541年1月10日与?在英国諾福爾可结婚。她去世于1566年7月11日在英国諾福爾可享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wayte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite ‘watchman, member of the watch’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Wayte was born in 1488, in Tittleshall, Norfolk, England, her father, William Wayte, was 34 and her mother, Margaret, was 29. She married ? from 11 January 1540 to 10 January 1541, in Norfolk, England. She died on 11 July 1566, in Norfolk, England, at the age of 78.

Elle a épousé ? du 11 janvier 1540 au 10 janvier 1541, à Norfolk, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 11 juillet 1566, à Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans.
